channel.title: BUbuisnessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbuisnessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump’s tariffs are a ‘dirty tax’ that will make the $38.6 trillion national debt crisis even worse over the long term, top analyst says
item.description: "The idea that this is pro-American is actually just the opposite," Penn Wharton's Kent Smetters told Fortune. "It hurts American manufacturers."

item.title: American Express and Visa alumni raise $4 million for Rhythmic, which partners with brands to provide financial products for their customers
item.description: Startup aims to make stablecoins more accessible to the general public.

item.title: From mom-and-pops to Goldman Sachs, Jesse Jackson’s vision for American business sparked a revolution
item.description: The civil rights leader, whose dealmaking skills pushed Wall Street to invest in minority-owned businesses, died on Tuesday.

item.title: This Fortune 500 CEO says ‘lean manufacturing’—the management philosophy Toyota made famous—is a prerequisite for leveraging AI
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: It’s no time to go quiet on sustainability. Just ask beauty giant L’Oreal
item.description: L’Oréal is betting millions that sustainability can drive profits as well as protect the planet.

item.title: Your stocks are becoming 24/7 digital cash
A stock-market strategy that combines value, quality and momentum
U.S. first-time jobless claims fall to lowest level so far this year
Etsy on track for 20% rally after sale of Depop unit to eBay
U.S.’s trade deficit for 2025 was its third biggest ever
